thesis: The best-positioned hyperscaler in the new capital-intensity regime: 18% growth, $627B backlog, Azure +40%, and a ~$190B capex program funded entirely from operating cash flow with zero equity raise, at 20.5x forward — below its own 5-year trough range. Bear risk is macro multiple compression (Dec hike odds ~43%, $91 oil) and $37B FCF-after-capex (~80x FCF) if AI revenue per megawatt disappoints.

Cheapest multiple in the Mag7 (15.8x forward) for its fastest grower (+33% revenue, 82% gross margin), but the market is now grading capital intensity and META screens worst: $125-145B capex with no cloud to monetize it, FCF-after-capex heading toward zero, and a rumored tens-of-billions equity raise. High-torque setup: funding clarity re-rates it toward $850-900; a large dilutive raise into this tape retests $520.

The ad engine is intact (13 straight double-digit quarters, +30% in Q1) and the 2H26 game pipeline is on schedule, but our May bullishness was early — the stock fell 19% as back-end-loaded catalysts offered no defense in a risk-off China-ADR tape. At 13.4x forward with ~26% of market cap in net cash, BILI is a show-me story whose first hard checkpoint is NCard's official launch in July; until a game actually ships and charts, the multiple stays hostage to ADR flows.

Still the cheapest highly-profitable automaker — 5.6x forward earnings, 9.4% operating margin, $9-11B FCF, and a $6B buyback retiring ~8% of the float — but the June 10 -5.2% rejection at the all-time-high zone, a ~$50M late-May C-suite insider-sale cluster led by CEO Barra, and $91 oil (gasoline +40% y/y) aimed at the truck/SUV engine that drives 83% of EBIT argue for buying weakness toward $72-76 rather than chasing; 12-month base case $92.

The value-trap pattern is now confirmed: a third guidance cut (FY26 EPS to $10.95-11.15, North America down high-single-digits) and management-admitted brand/product misfires against Alo and Vuori mean the 10x multiple is cheap for a reason; international +22%, an 8.4% FCF yield, and Heidi O'Neill's September 8 CEO start are real options, but wait for evidence of NA stabilization — not price — before re-engaging.

A $1.4B treasury (~65% of market cap) attached to a photonics roll-up and a quantum-narrative call option: Q1'26 revenue of $3.69M was ~95% acquired (organic ~$204K) with negative gross margin, share count grew 64% in a year, and a June dilution-related shareholder vote lands in the most dilution-hostile tape of the year — strictly a 0.5-1.5% lottery ticket with a $4.40-6.20/share cash floor, 29.7% short interest squeeze fuel, and sovereign-quantum-stake/defense-contract headlines as the only true upgrades.

The June 4 -14% crash was guidance anchoring, not demand breakage: AI semis grew 143% and Broadcom booked >$30B of new AI orders even as the ~$16B Q3 AI guide missed the whisper. At 19.2x forward for a contracted custom-XPU plus networking franchise with VMware software annuity, the reset looks like opportunity — but the stock now trades on AI capex financing sentiment, not fundamentals.

At 15.7x forward earnings with Blackwell sold out through 2026 and Rubin promising a 10x token-cost reduction, NVDA is cheap on numbers — but the market is now grading hyperscaler capex financing (ORCL's $40B raise, debt-funded buildouts), not chip demand. Export-control overhang on China and the macro rate-hike scare keep the multiple compressed despite 85%+ growth.

The CSRC penalty (~$60M, booked in Q1's GAAP loss; CEO personally fined) is paid but the crackdown is active, not resolved: mainland clients hold ~10% of the $58.9B client assets and enter a mandated two-year sell-only wind-down while regulators vow to 'fully eradicate' non-compliant cross-border brokerage. Against that, the other 90% of the franchise is compounding (Q1 revenue +26%, client assets +28% YoY, SG/HK-led account growth), the stock trades at 0.99x book with a $50M buyback live, and a Fed hike would actually raise interest income — post-kitchen-sink value with an unpriceable regulatory left tail.

The existential balance-sheet question has materially eased — RMB48.2B cash, positive operating cash flow and net current assets, 18.8% vehicle margin, two straight non-GAAP profitable periods, and the EV trio's best growth (May deliveries +62% YoY) — yet the stock trades at ~0.9x sales on an execution discount earned by years of misses. The June 9 DoD 'Chinese military companies' (1260H) designation adds a fresh policy overhang on a razor-thin 0.2% profit margin, so the re-rate to consensus $7 requires both a clean Q2 and a calmer ADR flow regime.

May revenue +30.1% y/y and 3nm price hikes of up to 15% confirm TSM's AI-wafer monopoly pricing power, and it was the relative-strength winner of the June semis crash. But a -13% global smartphone year hits ~26% of revenue, the capex supercycle suppresses near-term FCF, and the Taiwan geopolitical tail plus tariff risk keep the risk premium structurally fat at 20.9x forward.

Broadest international L4 autonomous franchise (2,800 vehicles, 12 countries) with fresh Madrid and Slovakia wins and ~44% of market cap in cash, but trading at all-time lows because the persistent China-ADR delisting overhang, >$200M/yr burn with no breakeven guidance, and a rate-hike-fearing extreme-fear tape all punish exactly this profile; hold/small accumulate below $6.50 with a 12-month base case of $9.50.

Cloud/AI is genuinely accelerating (+38% cloud, AI revenue triple-digit for 11 straight quarters) but FY26 non-GAAP net income fell 62% and FCF swung to a RMB46.6B outflow as instant-commerce subsidies and a RMB380B capex plan consume the P&L. At 12.5x forward earnings the stock is optically cheap, yet the gap-closing catalysts (quick-commerce breakeven end-FY3/27, AI crossing 50% of cloud mix) are quarters away while HFCAA overhang, Fed-hike odds, and $91 oil keep China ADR flows hostile.

Operationally the L4 robotaxi story keeps delivering (Q1'26 revenue +145%, robotaxi +395%, raised 3,500-vehicle target, EU 17-state framework backing its Zagreb beachhead), but at ~23x EV/S the stock is pure duration in a rate-hike-fearing tape — down 21% in a week to within 2% of its 52-week low, with options flow flipped bearish and the Nov 2026 founder-lockup expiry ahead; hold, staged accumulation only below $7.50, 12-month base case $13.

The only full-stack AI company (TPU silicon, Gemini, Cloud +63% on a $462B backlog) just pre-funded its $180-190B capex with an $84.75B equity raise anchored by $10B from Berkshire — at the cost of a four-week, -13% slide that puts the stock exactly at its 5-year-average multiple. Fair value with a fat bull tail (TPU externalization) against two real risks: the DOJ cross-appeal seeking Chrome divestiture/payment bans, and another raise cycle if 2027 capex climbs again.

MU was the epicenter of both the HBM supercycle and the June 4-5 crash (-20% in two days; memory ETF -15%): the entire 2026 HBM slate is sold out under binding 3-5 year LTAs and FQ3 guidance ($33.5B rev, 81% GM, $19.15 EPS, June 24 print) is historic, but at 8x potentially-peak forward EPS, P/S 5x history, $25B+ rising capex in a regime that now punishes capital intensity, and consensus already above guide, the scenario tree (~$885 blended) is fully priced at $892.

Q1 confirmed the bear case — vehicle margin collapsed to 6.1% (vs NIO's 18.8%) as the cheaper i6 BEV reached ~60% of mix, driving a RMB2.29B loss on -11.4% revenue — and the stock sits at fresh 52-week lows with the Street cutting targets. What remains is a falsifiable trough thesis: ~$10.30/ADS net cash (~75% of market cap) as a leaky floor, plus the L9/L8 refresh and management's explicit ~10% Q2 gross-margin guide to be graded at the late-August print; entry before that proof point is paying for a promise Li has not yet kept.

Operationally flawless defensive compounder — +11.6% Q3 revenue, 9.8% comps, renewal rate UP to 92.2% post-fee-hike, and record gas volumes that turn the oil shock into a traffic weapon — but at 43.5x forward EPS with December rate-hike odds at ~43%, the multiple remains the entire bear case; the 11% post-earnings flush to the 5Y-average multiple only partially de-risks it.

Record 20.6% gross margin and real physical-AI optionality (Q3 Guangzhou robotaxi pilot, VLA 2.0, AeroHT HK IPO, Magna-built EU expansion) are pitted against a shrinking core: Q1 revenue fell 17.6% YoY with a swing back to a RMB1.8B loss and May deliveries -4% YoY on a Mona-heavy mix. At 25.8x forward — the richest multiple of the EV trio while testing 52-week lows — the stock needs the GX SUV ramp and on-time robotaxi pilot to re-accelerate deliveries before the AI premium is justified.

Inflection-point insurtech with 71% revenue growth, ten straight quarters of accelerating IFP, and a 16pp loss-ratio improvement to 62% — now FCF-positive and monetizing autonomy via its June Indiana Tesla-FSD insurance launch, yet still binary on the Q4 2026 adj-EBITDA breakeven. At $55.86 in a fear-regime tape (beta 1.81, 14.6% short interest, rising 10Y compressing its 5.1x sales multiple), base case is $69 (+24%) if breakeven lands, $38 if it slips.

The regime winner on the metric the market now grades — $101B FCF on just ~$39B capex, zero dilution risk — which is why AAPL is the only Mag7 name above both moving averages in this tape. But you pay 30.4x forward (85th percentile of its own history) for mid-single-digit organic growth, IDC's -13% smartphone-unit 2026 forecast, and a WWDC AI Siri that shipped as an English-only beta with no EU/China availability: a safe-haven multiple, not a growth multiple.

The narrative is advancing — full Austin-metro robotaxi (Jun 4) after Dallas/Houston, FSD subs +51% to 1.28M, $44.7B cash and zero dilution risk — but the numbers are the Mag7's weakest: 4.2% operating margin, ~272x FCF, energy storage deployments -15% y/y, and a 152.6x forward multiple above its own 5-year ceiling in a market that just repriced ORCL/SMCI for exactly this profile. Even a bull DCF (28% growth, 13% FCF margin) only reaches $307; at $381 this is a venture bet on robotaxi/Optimus in a 1.8-beta wrapper — size it accordingly.

Seagate is the rare AI-infrastructure name the new regime should reward: nearline exabytes are sold out through CY2026 under LTAs stretching into 2027-28, the HAMR/Mozaic density roadmap grows capacity without a capex arms race, and it was the only crash casualty to get post-crash price-target RAISES (Mizuho to $1,090 June 8) — but at 30.5x forward EPS and ~16.6x sales versus a 5Y average near 10x earnings, with the CEO selling ~$50M in two weeks, the $780-880 fair-value band brackets the $816 spot and the easy re-rating is behind it.

The June 4-10 semi crash (-17% in five sessions) reset AMD from 38x to 34.6x forward earnings — near its 5Y average — while the contracted MI400/Helios demand stack (Meta ~6GW, OpenAI 6GW, Oracle 50K GPUs) is unchanged and AMD's fab-light, net-cash model sidesteps the capital-intensity penalty now hitting AI infrastructure names; upside still hinges entirely on the Q3 2026 Helios rack ramp shipping on time into a fear-regime macro with December Fed-hike odds at ~43%.

A -23% week purged the ByteDance data-center euphoria, leaving QCOM at 17.9x forward with consensus PT ($180) now below the price — the Street doesn't believe the AI pivot yet. Auto (+38%) and IoT diversification are real, but handsets (~57% of revenue) face IDC's -13% smartphone year and NVIDIA's RTX Spark attacks the Windows-on-Arm flank; the June 24 Investor Day and December hyperscaler shipment are the binary catalysts.

The foundry turnaround is no longer a narrative — six straight beats, GM 29.7%→39.4%, and an 18A order book of Apple, Microsoft, AWS, Tesla, and now Google (3M+ TPUs, June 8, +11.2%) with NVIDIA running trials, making INTC the only AI-semi to reclaim its breakout after the June 4-5 crash; but at 69.6x forward EPS, 10x sales, -$8.3B FCF and consensus PT ($92) below spot, $107 already prices a 50-60% chance of the full foundry-duopoly bull case in a regime that punishes capital intensity.
